items.title: Injuries and fires reported after NT cracker night celebrations

children.content: People across the Northern Territory have spent the night setting off their own fireworks for the Territory's annual cracker night celebrations, lighting up the sky with colour and keeping firefighters busy putting out blazes.

gallery.altText: green and pink fireworks light up the sky over water with a crowd on the shore watching
red fireworks going off over the ocean
Three children wave orange flags with fireworks going off in the background

thumbnail.altText: green and pink fireworks light up the sky over water with a crowd on the shore watching
red fireworks going off over the ocean
Three children wave orange flags with fireworks going off in the background

items.title: Former St Mary's Hostel site recognised as key Stolen Generations place

children.content: Former residents say the heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

image.altText: A woman in a light grey jumper wearing sunglasses stands in front of a large tree

items.title: Alice Springs drug and alcohol rehab curbed due to flooding

children.content: Drug and Alcohol Services Australia is running programs at a vastly reduced capacity due to flooding at its Alice Springs site earlier this year.

image.altText: The camera looks across the street at the DASA site behind a green fence, with cars out the front on the curb.

items.title: Pair behind dramatic flood rescue awarded for going 'beyond the call of duty'

children.content: A helicopter pilot and a police officer who performed a daring flood rescue in Katherine earlier this year have been awarded for their actions as part of the Northern Territory's Territory Day awards.

image.altText: Man in high-vis sits on top of a 4WD vehicle half submerged in floodwater, holding a dog, as a helicopter hovers next to them.

items.title: NT power, water and sewerage prices increase by 5.3 per cent from today

children.content: The Northern Territory treasurer has defended an increase in power, water and sewerage prices, which are set to rise by 5.3 per cent from today.

image.altText: Power lines on a leafy green street.

items.title: Today in history: NT town of Katherine gazetted 100 years ago

children.content: Today marks 100 years of the NT town of Katherine, which has a rich history as a meeting place and crossroads to the north and as home to some of Australia's most stunning natural sites.

gallery.altText: A group of Russian men and women are sitting on a tractor in a historical photo

thumbnail.altText: A group of Russian men and women are sitting on a tractor in a historical photo

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: 'Recurring issues' uncovered following surveillance of chopper sector

children.content: Australia's aviation watchdog has warned that cutting corners on maintenance and certification requirements could create a "real safety risk".

image.altText: A chopper shot from below, it is parked on green grass and a blue sky can be seen in the background.

items.title: 'Hyperscale' data centre planned for remote NT

children.content: The proposed 19,150-hectare 'Project Ares' would be built by Energy North on Murranji Station in the Barkly Region, as up to 12 proponents eye the NT for data centres.

image.altText: a gate with a cut-out of a man on a horse silhouetted against a sunset.

items.title: Study finds NT residents are the nation's happiest

children.content: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

image.altText: A woman, smiling, standing in a CBD mall.

items.title: 'Kick in the guts': NT veterans join calls for health funding cap removal

children.content: The federal government wants to cap access to physical and mental health supports to $5,000 a year for injured war veterans, but there are growing concerns the move will push some into "a worse-off situation".

image.altText: A group of people sitting around a table in a cafe, an older man is pointing his finger as he speaks to the group.

items.title: Teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible

children.content: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

gallery.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

thumbnail.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

items.title: 'Have to get me off at gunpoint': The fight for people's right to live on boats

children.content: For Australians on the brink of homelessness, dilapidated boats have become a way to stave off sleeping on the streets, but many are being removed from these vessels amid a safety crackdown.

image.altText: A man in a high-vis orange jacket standing on a boat.

items.title: World's biggest porn site puts advertising block on Australian AI empire

children.content: Australian pornography website OurDream has had its advertising suspended by Pornhub after the ABC revealed OurDream's content could include child exploitation material.

image.altText: A montage of profile photos of characters with blurred faces and age estiamtes beside them.

items.title: Christian Brothers granted pause on abuse victim payouts

children.content: The Christian Brothers religious order has been granted a moratorium on all civil claims against it, after arguing it was running out of money to pay victims of child sexual abuse.

image.altText: cross on a church steeple at night

items.title: Thousands of survivors of child sexual abuse face looming redress backlog

children.content: At its current processing rate, the scheme will not be able to clear its backlog before it closes.

image.altText: Chris Coombes

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play next and how to watch the knockout match

children.content: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Players line up and sing their national anthem.

articles.title: Mile Jedinak knows what it's like to take a World Cup penalty

children.content: Now an assistant coach for the Socceroos, and with a penalty shootout a possibility when Australia face Egypt in the round of 32, Mile Jedinak knows what it takes to seize the moment.

image.altText: Jedinak scores penalty against the Netherlands

children.content: Facing World Cup elimination at the hands of DR Congo, England needed a hero to step up when it mattered most. Harry Kane was that man.

image.altText: Harry Kane from behind holds out his hands in front of a crowd

children.content: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2. Follow live.

updates.title: FT: Ten-man USA beats Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0
79' USA has another goal ruled out!

items.title: Foreign nationals deported after reaching Far North Queensland by boat

children.content: The federal government says a group of foreign nationals has been removed from Australia after reaching Far North Queensland illegally by boat this week.

image.altText: CCTV footage shows two men ordering from Weipa bakery.

items.title: NSW sergeant 'snuck a look' for ex-cop mate accused of domestic violence

children.content: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

image.altText: Photo illustration with police officer silhouette and text messages from story

items.title: Quick hits: Feuding Belgians save the day, England claims 'disadvantage'

children.content: Belgium stars turn on each other before combining to save their team, England makes claims of "disadvantage", and Congo's goalkeeper gives it all he's got. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

articles.title: USA's World Cup dream alive despite red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina

children.content: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2.

items.title: Lethal Weapon actor Danny Glover says he has Alzheimer's

children.content: Danny Glover, who turns 80 in July,  has revealed that he has Alzheimer's disease and is leaning on his family for support as he lives with the incurable condition.

image.altText: Danny Glover is in a black suit and patterned bowtie, standing by a red wall with the word Oscars written on it.

items.title: 'Less water than one restaurant': AI data centre company responds to concerns

children.content: One of three AI factories planned for northern Tasmania would be quieter than a library to the nearest resident, Firmus co-CEO Oliver Curtis says, as the company moves to assuage community concerns around power use, water and noise.

image.altText: two men in dark tops stand against a red brick wall, one brunette and taller, the other with grey hair

items.title: Jackass began with a gunshot. Now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

items.title: Matthew Flinders’s steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

image.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Police rescue 'feral' children from squalor inside dilapidated US home

children.content: Sixteen children from the same family rescued from a dilapidated rural home in Ohio were living in wretched conditions with human waste all around, authorities say.

image.altText: A white, weatherboard home with a grey tin roof and green lawns surrounded by yellow police tape with a pile of rubbish.

items.title: ASX edges down while shoe retailer Betts shuts stores

children.content: The ASX edges down following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Picture of stock market board with ASX sign at the top left corner.

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum came from but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

image.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

items.title: Before and after images show widespread impact of Venezuelan quakes

children.content: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

gallery.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

items.title: US Navy crew member missing as chopper goes down in Arabian Sea

children.content: A US Navy service member is missing and three others are injured after their MH-60S Sea Hawk helicopter went down in the Arabian Sea.

image.altText: A US Navy grey fighter helicopter hovering above the deck of an aircraft carrier, where three people in jackets are standing.

items.title: Homicide detectives investigate death of teenager outside Melbourne hospital

children.content: The boy was found by emergency services workers with serious injuries outside a medical centre in Craigieburn, but died at the scene.

image.altText: A sign reads Craigieburn Community Hospital Northern Health Main Entry.

items.title: 'At greatest risk of CTE': Expert calls out dangers of heading the ball

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

gallery.altText: A soccer player in blue and orange heads a ball in front of trees in a park.

thumbnail.altText: A soccer player in blue and orange heads a ball in front of trees in a park.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair holding a microphone.

items.title: XPeng electric vehicle owners still chasing $5k cashback months on

children.content: When Mustansir Milky took the leap and bought a new electric vehicle, he didn't expect to have to fight to get the $5,000 cashback payment he was promised.

image.altText: Mustansir Milky stands beside his electric car outside his house

items.title: Broncos giant set for NFL shot as fellow prop's future 'uncertain'

children.content: The Brisbane Broncos are releasing forward Ben Te Kura from the rest of his NRL contract to allow him to pursue his dream of playing in the NFL.

image.altText: Ben Te Kura runs at Brisbane Broncos training.

items.title: Djokovic apologises for pranking ball kid during 'vintage' Wimbledon win

children.content: A relaxed Novak Djokovic takes time out between sets of his second-round Wimbledon win over Stefanos Tsitsipas to play a prank on a ball kid.

articles.title: 'My last Wimbledon': Kyrgios bows out with typically erratic doubles outing

children.content: Australian Nick Kyrgios says he has almost certainly played his final Wimbledon match after losing in the first round of the men's doubles alongside Alexander Bublik.

image.altText: Nick Kyrgios runs forward during a doubles match at Wimbledon. Alexander Bublik is behind him.

articles.title: Kasatkina trying to enter uncharted territory but Osaka stands in her way

children.content: Ahead of their third-round Wimbledon clash, Australian Daria Kasatkina is heartened to hear Naomi Osaka has, like her, never been past the third round at the All England Club.

image.altText: Daria Kastkina pumps her fist to celebrate at Wimbledon.

gallery.altText: Novak Djokovic pranks a ball kid at Wimbledon.

thumbnail.altText: Novak Djokovic pranks a ball kid at Wimbledon.

items.title: Spider venom spray could save Australia's honey bees from varroa mites

children.content: Queensland researchers are testing novel ways to kill varroa mites, which have developed a resistance to standard treatments.

image.altText: Two middle-aged men in a laboratory. One holds up a tank with a spider in it, the other a specimen jar.

items.title: Flooding damages endangered turtles' nesting sites and season

children.content: An environmental group says steep banks, loose sand and low water levels are resulting in extremely low clutch numbers.

image.altText: A small turtle entering a river.
